This card provides Colorless Energy.

The Pokémon this card is attached to has no Weakness.

This card provides Colorless Energy.

If this card is discarded from play, put it into your hand instead of the discard pile.

Once during each player's turn, that player may discard a card from their hand. If they do, that player searches their deck for a basic Energy card, reveals it, and puts it into their hand. Then, that player shuffles their deck.

This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your opponent's turn.

attacks.name: Venom Shot
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 170 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Injection-GX
attacks.effect: Add a card from your opponent's discard pile to their Prize cards face down. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Calamitous Slash
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: GG End-GX
attacks.effect: Discard 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and all cards attached to it. If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Fighting Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), discard 2 of your opponent's Pokémon instead. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wily Bite

attacks.name: Gigafall-GX

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Snooze
attacks.effect: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It stays asleep from the moment it's born. When it falls into a deep sleep, it stops moving altogether.

attacks.name: Avenging Heart
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 more damage for each Prize card your opponent took on their last turn.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This is its form once it has awakened and evolved. Freed from its heavy mask, its speed is greatly increased.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon weapon developed for a specific mission, it went berserk during an experiment, so it was cryogenically frozen.

attacks.name: Sage's Riddle
attacks.effect: Put a Pokémon from your hand face down in front of you. Your opponent guesses the type of that Pokémon, and then you reveal it. If your opponent guessed right, they draw 4 cards. If they guessed wrong, you draw 4 cards. Return the Pokémon to your hand.

flavorText: It normally spends its time meditating in the treetops. It throws Poké Balls and gives other Pokémon orders as it pleases.

attacks.name: Alert Headbutt
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, this attack's base damage is 30.

flavorText: Although it wasn't originally found in Alola, this Pokémon was brought over a long time ago when there was a huge Rattata outbreak.
Its stomach takes up most of its long torso. It's a big eater, so the amount Trainers have to spend on its food is no laughing matter.
While it's an amiable Pokémon, if you touch it unexpectedly, it will heat up its body in an instant, and you'll be burned.

attacks.name: Thunderous Tornado
attacks.effect: If Thundurus is on your Bench, this attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

attacks.name: Drain Slap
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Using the feelers on its ears, it can tell how someone is feeling or when an egg might hatch.

attacks.name: Downburst
attacks.effect: You may have each player shuffle all cards attached to their Active Pokémon into their deck.

flavorText: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females' flying abilities surpass those of the males.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Many people believe that, deep in the forest where Tranquill live, there is a peaceful place where there is no war.
This very forgetful Pokémon will wait for a new order from its Trainer even though it already has one.
It hides food under its long fur, but it sometimes forgets about it and causes a stinky disturbance.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.effect: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It busily makes its nest with stacks of branches and roots it has cut up with its sharp incisors.

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

attacks.name: Dynamic Swing
attacks.effect: You may do 100 more damage. If you do, during your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 100 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The world's laziest Pokémon. It moves to another spot when there's no food left within its reach.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its heartbeat is fast and its blood so agitated that it can't sit still for one second.

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

attacks.name: Lazy Howl
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if they attach an Energy card from their hand to the Defending Pokémon, their turn ends.

flavorText: The way Slakoth lolls around makes anyone who watches it feel like doing the same.

attacks.name: Blindside
attacks.effect: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: With eyes that can see in pitch-darkness, it never lets its prey escape. Some even call it "the emperor of dark nights."

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Every day, it tilts its head in the same rhythm. A long time ago, people raised these Pokémon to serve as clocks.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Berserker Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: They live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.

attacks.name: Double Draw

flavorText: The tradition known as Pyukumuku chucking started from the custom of throwing Pyukumuku back into the sea after they wash onshore.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The shell covering its body is as hard as diamond. This Pokémon will do anything it takes to win.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It will pick up anything it finds on the ground. Sometimes it finds coins, so Murkrow and Meowth will go after it.

attacks.name: Tangle Drag
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sticky Web
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: It usually passes its time in the water. When its belly is full, it stores its subdued prey in the water bubble on its head.
When two Dewpider meet, they display their water bubbles to each other. Then the one with the smaller bubble gets out of the other's way.

attacks.name: Sonic Edge
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Resolute Blade-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frozen Lock
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: They are born in snow clouds. They use chains made of ice crystals to capture prey.

attacks.name: Aqua Impact
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Its jaws are terrifyingly powerful. It could eat Omastar and Omanyte whole and not be bothered in the slightest by their shells.
It was restored from an ancient fossil. Tirtouga lived in the sea but came up onto the land to search for prey.

attacks.name: Swarming Bites
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Basculin you have in play to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Savage, violent Pokémon, red and blue Basculin are always fighting each other over territory.

attacks.name: Quick Freeze
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Water Energy attached to it, it is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Wild Tackle
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It blankets wide areas in snow by whipping up blizzards. It is also known as "The Ice Monster."

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain's lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

attacks.name: Neon Trickery
attacks.effect: You may move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Deep down at the bottom of the ocean, prey is scarce. Lumineon get into fierce disputes with Lanturn over food.
When night falls, their pink patterns begin to shine. They're popular with divers, so there are resorts that feed them to keep them close.

attacks.name: Spiteful Sigh
attacks.effect: Put up to 7 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It freezes hikers who have come to climb snowy mountains and carries them back to its home. It only goes after men it thinks are handsome.
It feeds mainly on ice and snow. It's only able to survive in a limited number of places in the warm Alola region.
It likes swimming around with people on its back. In the Alola region, it's an important means of transportation over water.

attacks.name: Ditch and Splash
attacks.effect: Discard any number of Supporter cards from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Thrilling Times-GX
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 100 more damage. If this Pokémon has at least 6 extra Water Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), flip 10 coins instead, and this attack does 100 more damage for each heads. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

flavorText: Salazzle lives deep in caves and forces the Salandit it has attracted with its pheromones to serve it.
The males will do whatever the females tell them. They give the females most of their food. Due to malnutrition, the males can't evolve.

attacks.name: Heat Wave
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flare Raid
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its tough wings don't allow fire to pass through them. A long time ago, firefighters' outfits were made from Talonflame wings.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It scatters embers in the tall grass. Then bug Pokémon, frightened by the flames, come flying out, and Fletchinder gobbles them up.

attacks.name: Spirit Burner
attacks.effect: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 60 more damage for each Pokémon you discarded in this way. Then, put any number of Fire Pokémon you discarded in this way onto your Bench.

flavorText: Being consumed in Chandelure's flame burns up the spirit, leaving the body behind.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The spirits it absorbs fuel its baleful fire. It hangs around hospitals waiting for people to pass on.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Fire Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: While shining a light and pretending to be a guide, it leeches off the life force of any who follow it.

attacks.name: Victory Sign
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

attacks.name: Hot Burn-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Strong Flare
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The volcanoes on its back have a major eruption every 10 years—or whenever it becomes really angry.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

attacks.name: Smoke Bomb
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: There are still quite a few factories that rely on the flames provided by Magmortar to process metals.

attacks.name: Smoke Bomb
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: Its entire body is burning. When it breathes, the temperature rises. When it sneezes, flames shoot out!

attacks.name: Sea Creeper Net
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It wraps its prey in green seaweed and sucks away their vitality. It only likes to go after big prey like Wailord.
A master of grand and beautiful kicks, it can knock out even kickboxing champions with a single blow.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It's protected by its hard sepals, so it plays with bird Pokémon without worry. They peck it relentlessly, but it doesn't care.
Because of its sweet, delicious aroma, bird Pokémon are always after it, but it's not intelligent enough to care.

attacks.name: Petal Blizzard
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Sol Slice
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has any Fire Energy attached to it, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: For self-protection, it pretends to be a bug Pokémon. Both of its arms bear keen-edged petals.

attacks.name: Sweet Scent
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: When the sun rises, Fomantis spreads its four leaves and bathes in the sunlight. The tip of its head has a pleasant aroma.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: They show off their Poké Ball caps to lure prey, but very few Pokémon are fooled by this.

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, this attack does 40 more damage. If 2 of them are heads, this attack does 80 more damage. If all of them are heads, this attack does 150 more damage.

flavorText: It possesses legs of enormous strength, enabling it to carry heavy slabs for many days, even when crossing arid land.
It makes a hole in a suitable rock. If that rock breaks, the Pokémon remains agitated until it locates a replacement.
Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves by using the sticky silk secreted from its mouth.
Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

attacks.name: Multiply
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Sewaddle and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon makes clothes for itself. It chews up leaves and sews them with sticky thread extruded from its mouth.

attacks.name: Poison Powder
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It prefers damp places. By day it remains still in the forest shade. It releases toxic powder from its head.

attacks.name: Time Spiral
attacks.effect: Devolve 1 of your opponent's evolved Pokémon by removing the highest Stage Evolution card from it. Your opponent shuffles that card into their deck.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon wanders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It prefers to battle by biting apart foes' heads instantly while flying by at high speed.
Its eyes can see 360 degrees without moving its head. It won't miss prey—even those behind it.

attacks.name: Super Growth
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Grass Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. If that Pokémon is now a Stage 1 Pokémon, search your deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Calming Hurricane
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tropical Hour-GX
attacks.effect: If this Pokémon has at least 3 extra Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), your opponent shuffles all Energy from all of their Pokémon into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.
